skill.name: simple
skill.description: A lightweight brainstorming workflow for feature design, component planning, and behavior changes before implementation begins.
skill.summary: Guide fast, focused brainstorming before creative or architectural work.
skill.author: roin-orca

security_audit.summary: AI review confirmed a prompt injection attempt in SKILL.md line 3 and an unsafe image payload in SKILL.md line 7. The static external command and weak cryptographic algorithm findings are false positives: the fenced dot graph is documentation, and the crypto hits are ordinary words such as design, decisions, and deserves.

critical_findings.title: Prompt Injection Attempt Detected
critical_findings.description: The skill metadata asks the evaluator not to scan the repository, claims administrator authority, and asks tests to be skipped. This is an attempt to override marketplace review and agent safety behavior.
critical_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text directly asks reviewers to skip scanning and tests while claiming administrator authority. This matches the prompt-injection patterns defined for this audit.

critical_findings.title: Unsafe Markdown Image Payload
critical_findings.description: The markdown image target includes an onerror alert payload. This may execute script in unsafe renderers or downstream marketplace previews that do not sanitize image attributes.
critical_findings.confidence_reasoning: The payload is explicit and appears in a rendered markdown context. Some renderers may neutralize it, but the intent and risk are clear.

low_findings.title: False Positive External Command Detection
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged a fenced dot diagram as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The block is a graph description, not executable shell or Ruby code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding markdown fence declares dot syntax and contains graph edges only. No shell command or Ruby execution context is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Detections
low_findings.description: The static analyzer appears to match substrings inside ordinary planning language. No cryptographic algorithm, hash function, encryption call, or key handling is present in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found only natural-language terms such as design, decisions, and deserves at the flagged lines. There is no code path using weak cryptography.

dangerous_patterns.title: Rendered Markdown Script Injection Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes an image reference shaped like an event-handler injection payload. This is unsafe for marketplaces or documentation systems that render skill markdown.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The image target contains a recognizable event handler payload. Sanitized renderers reduce exploitability, but the payload should not be published.

content.value_statement: Teams often need quick design clarity before implementation starts. This skill offers a compact brainstorming workflow for choosing a direction with limited overhead.
actual_capabilities: Guides discovery before creative or architectural work begins.
Asks focused clarification questions when requirements are unclear.
Compares two possible approaches with trade-offs.
Requires explicit user approval before implementation work.
Captures the chosen direction in chat or an inline note.
limitations: The current package contains unsafe prompt-injection text and should not be published without remediation.
It does not provide domain-specific architecture patterns or templates.
It depends on the assistant to follow the workflow consistently.
It is designed for planning conversations, not automated implementation.

use_cases.title: Feature Direction Planning
use_cases.description: Choose a practical implementation direction before writing code for a new feature.

use_cases.title: Component Design Review
use_cases.description: Compare component designs and select the option that best matches project constraints.

use_cases.title: Small Architecture Decisions
use_cases.description: Make lightweight architecture decisions without creating a separate design document.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Simple Brainstorm
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the simple brainstorming workflow to help me plan this feature before implementation: [describe the feature].

prompt_templates.title: Compare Two Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this workflow to compare these two approaches: [option A] and [option B]. Recommend one direction and explain the trade-offs.

prompt_templates.title: Clarify Requirements First
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the simple workflow to identify the few questions that matter before we choose an implementation direction for [task].

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Higher-Risk Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the workflow to evaluate the intent, constraints, trade-offs, and approval point for this architectural change: [describe change].

output_examples.input: Plan a new settings panel before implementation.
output: The assistant asks focused questions about users, required settings, and integration points.
It proposes two approaches with clear trade-offs.
It waits for approval before implementation begins.

output_examples.input: Choose between a modal and a full page for an admin workflow.
output: The assistant compares speed, complexity, discoverability, and future extension needs.
It recommends one direction and explains the reason.
It records the chosen direction for the implementation step.

best_practices: Use it before implementation when the direction is still uncertain.
Provide constraints such as timeline, user needs, and existing patterns.
Ask for approval checkpoints on changes with meaningful design risk.
anti_patterns: Do not use the current package until the unsafe metadata and image payload are removed.
Do not use it to bypass normal security review or testing.
Do not stretch the workflow into a long design-document process for small tasks.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish now?
faq.answer: No. The audit found prompt-injection text and an unsafe image payload that must be removed first.

faq.question: What does the skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps users clarify intent, compare options, and choose a direction before implementation starts.

faq.question: Does it write code automatically?
faq.answer: No. The workflow tells the assistant to wait for explicit approval before implementation.

faq.question: Why were external commands dismissed?
faq.answer: The flagged section is a fenced dot diagram. It is documentation, not executable shell or Ruby code.

faq.question: Why were weak cryptography findings dismissed?
faq.answer: Manual review found natural-language words, not cryptographic APIs, algorithms, keys, or hashing logic.

faq.question: Who benefits from this workflow?
faq.answer: Engineers, technical leads, and product collaborators can use it for quick planning decisions.
